commit.message:
feat: Maglev SSA optimizations (closes #314) (#377)

Add four new optimization passes to the Maglev mid-tier JIT compiler:

- Type specialization from IC feedback (type_specialization.rs):
  Replaces generic arithmetic nodes with typed fast-path equivalents
  (CheckedSmi*, Float64*, Int32*) based on inline-cache feedback.

- Range analysis (range_analysis.rs):
  Tracks integer [min, max] intervals and replaces CheckedSmi* nodes
  with unchecked Int32* equivalents when the output provably fits i32.

- Loop-invariant code motion (licm.rs):
  Detects natural loops via back-edges and hoists pure invariant nodes
  to the loop preheader.

- Type guards with deoptimization bailout (type_guards.rs):
  Inserts CheckSmi/CheckNumber guards before unguarded typed arithmetic,
  ensuring speculation correctness with deopt fallback.

All passes are integrated into the optimizer pipeline and include
comprehensive test suites (139 maglev tests passing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle broken pipe during upload, detect sparse files

The upload was failing at ~10.5 GB because:
1. The raw image is sparse (1.2 GB on disk, 35 GB logical size).
   We were sending the full 35 GB including zero blocks, hitting the
   Rancher nginx proxy body-size limit (~10 GB by default).
2. Python3's conn.send() raised BrokenPipeError but we didn't catch it
   or read the server response, so the error was opaque.

Changes:
- Pre-upload sparse-file warning: if logical size > 5× disk usage,
  warn the user and suggest compacting with qemu-img before uploading.
- Catch BrokenPipeError during streaming; after the break, try to read
  whatever response the server sent and print it.
- On broken-pipe failure, print actionable hints: proxy size limit and
  qemu-img compact command.

To compact a sparse image before uploading:
  qemu-img convert -f raw -O raw input.raw compact.raw

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add `c4 install` command for one-command setup

Embed all .c4/ templates as heredocs inside c4.sh so the script is
fully self-contained. Running `c4 install [path]` bootstraps the
entire .c4/ structure and copies c4.sh into the target folder —
no manual file copying required.

- Add cmd_install with embedded AGENTS.md, roster.md, config, all
  ROLE.md and PROFILE.md templates, and events log
- Guard against installing into an already-initialised folder
- Update cmd_help to document the new command
- Update README Quick Start and Install Globally sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace curl upload with Python3 streaming to fix OOM and 403

curl -T -X POST sends an 'Expect: 100-continue' header which some servers
(including Harvester via Rancher proxy) reject with 403 before the body
is even sent. curl --data-binary buffers the entire file in RAM (OOM on
large images).

Replace the binary upload in _do_upload() with Python3 (http.client):
- Streams in 4 MB chunks via conn.send() — no in-memory buffering
- No Expect: 100-continue header sent
- Shows percentage, MB, and MB/s progress on stderr
- Prints the server error body on failure for easier diagnosis

Python3 is already required by _kc_field(), so no new dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
